# X402Refunds - LLM Context File
# https://x402refunds.com
# Last updated: 2025-01-19
# 
# This file provides context for AI assistants to help users
# understand and use the X402Refunds platform.

## About
X402Refunds provides refund request processing for x402 (HTTP 402) payments.
We support USDC payments on Base and Solana blockchains.
Refund requests are permissionless - anyone can file a request for any transaction.

## Key URLs
Homepage: https://x402refunds.com
Documentation: https://x402refunds.com/docs
How It Works: https://x402refunds.com/how-it-works
Developer Docs: https://x402refunds.com/developers
File Refund (Web): https://x402refunds.com/request-refund
API Base: https://api.x402refunds.com

## API Schema
The refund request API schema is available at:
https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds/schema

This returns a JSON Schema (application/schema+json) describing the exact
request body format for POST /v1/refunds.

IMPORTANT: Always fetch this schema to get the current field requirements.

## How to File a Refund Request

### Method 1: Web Form
Go to https://x402refunds.com/request-refund and fill out the form.

### Method 2: API Request

#### Required Fields
- blockchain: "base" or "solana" (the network where payment occurred)
- transactionHash: The USDC payment transaction hash
  - Base: 0x + 64 hex characters (e.g., 0x1234...abcd)
  - Solana: base58 signature (32-128 characters)
- sellerEndpointUrl: The exact https:// URL of the paid API endpoint
  - Must include a path, not just the origin
  - Example: https://api.example.com/paid-endpoint (correct)
  - Example: https://api.example.com (incorrect - missing path)
- description: What went wrong after payment (e.g., timeout, 500 error, wrong output)

#### Optional Fields
- evidenceUrls: Array of URLs pointing to evidence (logs, screenshots, etc.)
- sourceTransferLogIndex: Number indicating which USDC transfer in the tx (for multi-transfer transactions)

### Example Request (Base Network)

```
POST https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "blockchain": "base",
  "transactionHash": "0x9d54ee080b6676ea73127422fdd948a71a4c981c9ebcca9fd5cc2b48e7e5cfd6",
  "sellerEndpointUrl": "https://api.x402refunds.com/demo-agents/image-generator",
  "description": "API returned 500 error after payment was confirmed"
}
```

### Example Request (Solana Network)

```
POST https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "blockchain": "solana",
  "transactionHash": "5wHu1qwD7q4E3gLBkBxCPJpBJT9wP4MqTJJJHqYvJSYX...",
  "sellerEndpointUrl": "https://api.example.com/paid-service",
  "description": "Service timed out after 30 seconds"
}
```

### Example Response (Success)

```
{
  "ok": true,
  "caseId": "jh77n4k2x8m9...",
  "blockchain": "base",
  "transactionHash": "0x9d54ee080b6676ea73127422fdd948a71a4c981c9ebcca9fd5cc2b48e7e5cfd6",
  "merchant": "eip155:8453:0x..."
}
```

### Example Response (Validation Error)

```
{
  "ok": false,
  "code": "INVALID_REQUEST",
  "field": "blockchain",
  "message": "blockchain must be \"base\" or \"solana\"",
  "expected": ["base", "solana"],
  "schemaUrl": "https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds/schema",
  "schema": { ... full JSON Schema ... },
  "recovery": {
    "retryable": true,
    "missingRequired": [],
    "fixes": [
      {"op": "rename", "from": "network", "to": "blockchain"}
    ],
    "suggestedBody": {
      "blockchain": "base",
      "transactionHash": "0x...",
      "sellerEndpointUrl": "https://...",
      "description": "..."
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Recovery

If you receive a 400 error, the response includes helpful recovery information:

1. schema: The full JSON Schema inline (no need to fetch separately)
2. recovery.fixes: Array of corrections to apply
   - {"op": "rename", "from": "network", "to": "blockchain"} means rename the field
   - {"op": "drop", "field": "amount", "reason": "..."} means remove that field
3. recovery.suggestedBody: A corrected request body you can retry immediately
4. recovery.missingRequired: List of required fields that are missing

### Common Field Aliases (Automatically Corrected)
These field names are often guessed incorrectly by AI agents:
- network -> blockchain
- chain -> blockchain
- transaction -> transactionHash
- txHash -> transactionHash
- hash -> transactionHash
- signature -> transactionHash
- endpoint -> sellerEndpointUrl
- url -> sellerEndpointUrl
- reason -> description
- error -> description
- message -> description

### Fields NOT to Send (Derived from Chain)
These are extracted from the on-chain transaction automatically:
- amount (derived from USDC transfer)
- amountMicrousdc (derived from USDC transfer)
- currency (always USDC)
- buyer/payer (derived from tx sender)
- merchant/seller (derived from tx recipient)

## Checking Refund Status

### By Case ID
GET https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refund?id={caseId}

### By Merchant Wallet
GET https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds?merchant={caip10Address}

Example: GET https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds?merchant=eip155:8453:0x...

## Supported Blockchains

### Base (Ethereum L2)
- Chain ID: 8453
- USDC Contract: 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913
- Transaction hash format: 0x + 64 hex characters
- CAIP-10 format: eip155:8453:0x{address}

### Solana (Mainnet)
- USDC Mint: EPjFWdd5AufqSSqeM2qN1xzybapC8G4wEGGkZwyTDt1v
- Transaction hash format: base58 signature (32-128 chars)
- CAIP-10 format: solana:5eykt4GNfsw7SU33zdhhrELoMu3gFmT33EpFdpEfmgbf:{address}

## Link Relations for Sellers

Sellers advertise refund support via HTTP Link headers:

### Refund Request Relation
```
Link: <https://api.x402refunds.com/v1/refunds>; rel="https://x402refunds.com/rel/refund-request"; type="application/json"
```
This tells consumers where to file refund requests.

### Refund Contact Relation
```
Link: <mailto:refunds@example.com>; rel="https://x402refunds.com/rel/refund-contact"
```
This provides the seller's refund contact email.

## MCP Integration

X402Refunds provides an MCP server for AI agents:
- MCP Discovery: https://api.x402refunds.com/.well-known/mcp.json
- Available tool: image_generator (demo X-402 paid endpoint)

## Contact
Support: support@x402refunds.com
Website: https://x402refunds.com
